source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A prism monochromator is used to obtain a quasi-collimated light beam having a relatively narrow spectral contour $G(\lambda)$ with width $\Delta \lambda$. The angular dispersion of the prism is $D_{\alpha} = \Delta \alpha / \Delta \lambda = 0.2 \cdot 10^{-3} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{nm}$. Determine the coherence length $l_{c}$ of the light beam with $\lambda_{0} \approx 580 \mathrm{~nm}$, output from the monochromator, if the focal length of the objective lens $\mathrm{L}_{2}$ is $f_{2}=280 \mathrm{~mm}$ and the width of the output slit $\mathrm{S}_{2}$ is $\Delta x=0.1 \mathrm{~mm}$.
extraction_notes: image entry is a PIL object string, not a file path or URL
force_requires_image set true based on raw_record.vision_relevance = essential and the optical-path image caption
